title: Inviting Spring Norwegian Duck Stew
description: A hearty and earthy Norwegian duck stew featuring duck fat and ferment-jang, perfect for a weeknight dinner, this ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly dish is cooked in a fondue pot. It combines the richness of duck with the brightness of spring vegetables.
instructions: Heat the duck fat in the fondue pot over medium heat until melted.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chopped carrots and potatoes, cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the ferment-jang, stirring to combine, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the duck breast pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the spring peas, thyme, salt, and black pepper, stirring to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the duck is cooked through and the vegetables are tender.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh thyme if desired.
Store leftovers in the refrigerator for up to 3 days, reheating gently over low heat.
